name: Lightnin' Hopkins
description: American country blues singer, songwriter and guitarist (1912-1982)
abstract: Samuel John "Lightnin'" Hopkins (March 15, 1912 – January 30, 1982) was an American country blues singer, songwriter, guitarist and occasional pianist from Centerville, Texas. In 2010, Rolling Stone magazine ranked him No.

concerts.label: Hootenanny: Pete Seeger, Lightnin' Hopkins, Joan Baez, Clancy Brothers and Tommy Makem
Lightnin Hopkins, Bonnie Raitt, and Muddy Waters
Boogie n' Blues at Carnegie Hall

about_quotes.speaker: Bob Lovett
about_quotes.evidence: I like Bessie Smith and, uh, I even like, uh, a lot of folk artists, uh, you know, uh, jazz, basically jazz folk artists like Lightning Hopkins.

about_quotes.speaker: Ann Rabson
about_quotes.evidence: I was listening to Broonzy , I was listening to Lightnin’ Hopkins, Brownie McGhee, you know, these guys.
about_quotes.doc_title: Ann Rabson interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, September 21, 2007

about_quotes.speaker: Mose Allison
about_quotes.evidence: There were also the country blues people, like Muddy Waters, Lightnin’ Hopkins, and John Lee Hooker.
about_quotes.doc_title: Mose Allison — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Butch Cage and Robert Pete Williams
about_quotes.evidence: And my way of going, playing a guitar. So I played like Lightning Hopkins.
about_quotes.doc_title: Butch Cage and Robert Pete Williams Interview, 1967-05-13
